key_insight: OmniMatBench shows that current multimodal language models still struggle with materials-science reasoning, with the best model reaching only 0.372 overall performance across a broad expert-curated benchmark.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark helps identify where AI assistants can and cannot support materials discovery, processing, and application workflows in materials engineering.
method: Built 3,171 expert-curated QA and calculation problems
Covers 19 materials-science subfields across knowledge, processing, and applied materials
Evaluated 13 open-source and closed-source MLLMs under formula-, retrieval-, and code-assisted settings
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the best evaluated model achieved only 0.372 overall score, with strong performance variation across subfields and limited high-level knowledge application.
watch_item: Because the benchmark is broad and reasoning-focused, results may not directly translate to narrow property-prediction tasks or real-world lab deployment without domain-specific validation.
paper_title: OmniMatBench: A Human-Calibrated Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark Across 19 Materials Science Subfields
